breakdown: This PR widens the state flag semantics in game_state.py to accept numeric booleans (int 1/0, string '1'/'0') in addition to strict True/'true' and False/'false'. Tests are comprehensive, covering accepted/rejected edge cases and checking that bool is handled correctly as a subclass of int. The code has good documentation explaining the WHY (preventing silent level-up stalls from LLM numeric booleans). Minor deduction in error handling as no exceptions are involved, but type safety properly handles bool-as-int edge case.
key_changes: Widen _is_state_flag_true to accept int 1 and string '1' as truthy
Widen _is_state_flag_false to accept int 0 and string '0' as falsy
Add isinstance checks with bool exclusion to prevent bool(True)/int(1) confusion
Add comprehensive StateFlagSemanticsTest class with 5 test methods
Add rejection of free-text strings like 'yes'/'no' (not true/false)
